settings.title: One Breath to Steady a Racing Mind
settings.excerpt: You can borrow this simple breathing pattern from the mat and use it in a meeting or a queue.
settings.body:
People often tell us the calm they find in class evaporates the moment life gets loud again. The good news is that one of our most useful tools fits in your pocket.

You do not need a mat, a quiet room, or even a spare minute that looks like meditation. You only need your own breath, which you have with you everywhere.

The pattern itself

It is simply a longer exhale than inhale. Breathe in for a count of four, then out for a count of six. The slow out breath gently signals your body to settle.

That longer exhale is doing real work, nudging the part of your nervous system that handles rest rather than alarm. You are not imagining the calm. You are prompting it.

Where to use it

Try a few rounds before a difficult phone call, in traffic, or when you cannot fall asleep. Nobody around you will even notice.

A few moments that suit it well:

  • Right before you walk into something that makes you tense.
  • In a queue or a waiting room, with time to spare.
  • Lying awake at night with a busy head.

We practise this at the start of most Stillpoint classes precisely so it becomes second nature when you need it most. The more often you use it when calm, the more readily it comes when you are not.
